name: python_game_pipeline
description: 3-stage pipeline: generate engine → generate assets → assemble game

stages.name: game_engine
stages.prompt: Write a Python class 'GameEngine' with: a 10x10 grid, a player at position (0,0), methods move_up(), move_down(), move_left(), move_right() that update position (clamped to grid), and a display() method that prints the grid with 'P' at player position and '.' elsewhere. No explanation. Only code.

stages.name: game_items
stages.prompt: Extend the following GameEngine class to add: a list of 5 random items placed on the grid (represented by 'I'), a method collect_item() that removes the item if the player is on it and increments a score counter, and update display() to show items and score. No explanation. Only code.
stages.notes: Stage 2: inject engine output as context

stages.name: game_final
stages.prompt: Given the following game code, write a complete runnable main() function that: creates a GameEngine, runs a demo sequence of moves (up, right, right, down, left), calls collect_item() each step, and prints the final score. Make it a complete self-contained script. No explanation. Only code.
